prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments. Usage:

  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --user www-data --description 'My Application' --after network.target --restart on-failure --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args with defaults:
  --user (default: root)
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)
  --restart (default: on-failure, choices: no, on-success, on-failure, on-abnormal, on-watchdog, on-abort, always)
  --restart-sec (default: 5)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --wanted-by (default: multi-user.target)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs to add as Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (optional WorkingDirectory=)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections.

  "file_path": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-8djbpute/generate_service.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
from pathlib import Path


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, dest="exec_start",
                        help="Command to execute")

    # Optional arguments with defaults
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root",
                        help="User to run service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", default=None,
                        help="Service description (default: '{name} service')")
    parser.add_argument("--after", action="append", default=None,
                        help="Units to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart", default="on-failure",
                        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal",
                                 "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
                        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5,
                        help="Seconds to wait before restart (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", default=None,
                        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target",
                        help="Target to install into (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", default=[],
                        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", default=None,
                        help="Working directory for the service")

    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate systemd service unit file content."""
    # Apply defaults that depend on other args
    description = args.description or f"{args.name} service"
    after_units = args.after or ["network.target"]
    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"

    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={' '.join(after_units)}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")

    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")

    for env in args.env:
        lines.append(f"Environment={env}")

    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    output_path = Path(output_file)
    output_path.write_text(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
"

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments."""

import argparse
from pathlib import Path


def parse_args():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        description="Generate systemd service unit files"
    )

    # Required arguments
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, dest="exec_start",
                        help="Command to execute")

    # Optional arguments with defaults
    parser.add_argument("--user", default="root",
                        help="User to run service as (default: root)")
    parser.add_argument("--description", default=None,
                        help="Service description (default: '{name} service')")
    parser.add_argument("--after", action="append", default=None,
                        help="Units to start after (default: network.target, can specify multiple)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart", default="on-failure",
                        choices=["no", "on-success", "on-failure", "on-abnormal",
                                 "on-watchdog", "on-abort", "always"],
                        help="Restart policy (default: on-failure)")
    parser.add_argument("--restart-sec", type=int, default=5,
                        help="Seconds to wait before restart (default: 5)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", default=None,
                        help="Output file path (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument("--wanted-by", default="multi-user.target",
                        help="Target to install into (default: multi-user.target)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", default=[],
                        help="Environment variables as KEY=VALUE (repeatable)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", default=None,
                        help="Working directory for the service")

    return parser.parse_args()


def generate_service_file(args):
    """Generate systemd service unit file content."""
    # Apply defaults that depend on other args
    description = args.description or f"{args.name} service"
    after_units = args.after or ["network.target"]
    output_file = args.output or f"{args.name}.service"

    lines = []

    # [Unit] section
    lines.append("[Unit]")
    lines.append(f"Description={description}")
    lines.append(f"After={' '.join(after_units)}")
    lines.append("")

    # [Service] section
    lines.append("[Service]")
    lines.append(f"ExecStart={args.exec_start}")
    lines.append(f"User={args.user}")
    lines.append(f"Restart={args.restart}")
    lines.append(f"RestartSec={args.restart_sec}")

    if args.working_dir:
        lines.append(f"WorkingDirectory={args.working_dir}")

    for env in args.env:
        lines.append(f"Environment={env}")

    lines.append("")

    # [Install] section
    lines.append("[Install]")
    lines.append(f"WantedBy={args.wanted_by}")
    lines.append("")

    return "\n".join(lines), output_file


def main():
    args = parse_args()
    content, output_file = generate_service_file(args)

    output_path = Path(output_file)
    output_path.write_text(content)

    print(f"Generated: {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
